mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals impending recession risk, prompting institutional investors to reduce equity exposure within a single trading week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in consumer price inflation and federal funds rate hikes compresses equity risk premiums, driving negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline when both series rise concurrently, or interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding corporate capacity and robust order books, directly supporting earnings growth and driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative SPX returns in > 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts reflects tightening mortgage credit conditions and falling consumer confidence, depressing equity prices within days.
failure_condition: Housing starts drops do not predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal exhibits zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment confirms labor market strength, supporting consumer demand and equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint labor improvements fail to produce positive forward returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly proxies aggregate corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations adjust.
failure_condition: GDP growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward equity returns, or the signal reverses direction during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid federal funds rate hikes amid elevated consumer price inflation trigger risk-off repricing of duration-sensitive assets, depressing equity prices within a week.
failure_condition: Rate hikes during high inflation do not produce negative 5-day returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline.
